free_text: Total cross section of cadmium for neutrons of
the samples, analysis of the measurements and error
Thermal reactor, 24 MW(Th), radial beam channel.
Two types of sample used, either-
2. Cadmium metal plates of 99.9% purity and
Dummy samples used to correct for the sulphur
powder content of the first set of samples.
Correction for the sulphur in the Cd-S taking
its cross-section to be 1.25b at 0.178 eV.
Time dependent background was determined by a method
similar to that of Simpson et al (see REL-REF).
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. Subent 003 deleted (data
moved to Subent 005). Subent 003 deleted (the resonance
Errors in the energy scale were-
1.65% error in the flight path length giving
1.4 usec error in counter time Jitter giving
0.5 usec error in analyzer start time giving
an error of 0.9*sqrt(E) in the energy, error of less
than 0.005% in the analyzer frequency contributing
less than 0.01% to the energy scale error.
error due to impurities and content of S
error due to effect of grain size in
Assuming that the cross-section is a smooth function
of energy a parabola was fitted through 20
neighbouring points reducing the random error at
Both shape and area analyses applied using the
programs of atta and Harvey, USAEC report ORNL
for background due to other resonances.
Contribution to the total error from
Doppler width and potential scattering cross-section
are given in table 2 but all are considered
negligible compared with the standard deviation of
0.5% given by the shape analysis program.
isotopic abundance of Cd-113 from Nuclear

The resonance spin of J = 1 also taken from Nuclear
Standard deviations derived from the errors
in the resonance parameters used to obtain sigma-0.
These correspond mainly to the standard deviation
found from the shape analysis program.
No information about source of uncertainty
